The real action item: you're 10 commits behind origin. Pull first before doing anything else.


input.description: How many forge-doctrine files were in that commit

content.text:
Yeah — commit `0fa6f60` (`/forge-loop-aar`) dumped 58 `forge-doctrine/` files into this repo. Looks like an AAR skill run that blindly committed everything in the working directory.

That commit is already on `origin/main` (you're behind by 10). So the damage is upstream. Two options:

1. **Quick fix**: Delete `forge-doctrine/` from the tree and commit the removal. Simple, clean history going forward, no force push.
2. **Rewrite history**: Interactive rebase to remove it from `0fa6f60`. Cleaner history but requires a force push to `main`.

Given this is your repo, option 1 is the pragmatic call — one commit to nuke it, move the actual directory to the right place. Want me to do that?

input.description: Check if forge-doctrine is tracked in HEAD

content.text:
It was never committed to `main` — that `0fa6f60` commit was on a different branch or was reverted upstream. The directory was just sitting untracked in the working tree. Deleting it was all that was needed.

You're now up to date with `origin/main`, and `forge-doctrine/` is gone. Only thing left is the modified `Cargo.lock`:
